cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the writer's purpose for writing this passage. Let me read through the passage again and analyze the content. The passage starts with the writer recalling an event seven years prior when he was 13. He was in a social studies class when a teacher informed the class that the first World Trade Center building was hit by a plane. The class stopped work, turned on the TV, and watched the events. The school let them out early, and he went home, continuing to watch TV until bedtime. He mentions remembering the towers when walking to his Tae Kwon Do school, which was a big part of his life at the time. He describes the aftermath, the smoke, and the emptiness where the towers once stood.
